Without fault; guiltless ( be-taqsīr ). be-qusūr ṭhairānā , v. a. To pronounce innocent; to free; discharge ( barī k .).

A brother in his sister's house, a son-in-law at his father-in-law's and those who keep dogs, all are dogs. But a father in his daughter's house is a dog of dogs. 2. Kutte ke pāon jā aur billī ke pāoṅ ā! Prov. With a dog's legs fly, with a cat's tread homeward hie! 3. Jhūṭe hāth se kuttā bhī nahīṅ mārte . Prov. (A miser). He would n't even beat a dog off with the hand with which he was eating his dinner. (The scraps sticking to his hand would go to the dog). 4. Utrā meṅ bahe ūtī, ārīṅ baiṭhī jal pīye kūtī . Bhoj. Prov. The North wind from the North doth blow, the bitch drinks water where she sits. 2. A slave. Ādmī peṭ kā kuttā hai . Prov. Man is a dog (slave) to his stomach. 3. The belly. Yeh kuttā nahīṅ māṅtā . …

def: pron. (obl. kāhe), What? how? why? (The form is the same in both numbers, e.g. kyā hai, 'What is it?'; — kyā haiṅ, 'what are they?'; kyā is often the sign of interrogation at the beginning of a sentence, like the Persian āyā and the Latin an or num, and is, like them, untranslated when in such a position); — conj. Whether, or; — intj. What! how!
more: pron. S. किम Pr. kiṅṛā, keṅṛā . 1. What. 1. Kyā kahte ho ? What do you say? 2. Kyā āsmān ke tāre haiṅ! 3. Kyā kahūṅ! kuchh kahā nahiṅ jātā . 2. How much. 3. ( ak ) Whether; or; either. 1. Tum jāoge, kyā nahīṅ? 2. Shagl behtar hai ishq-bāzī kā, Kyā haqīqī o kyā majāzī kā . Walī. 4. As though. Kyā ghar kī bāt hai ? 5. Not. Woh kyā jāëgā? He wont go. kyā bāt hai, kyā kahne haiṅ! intj. It goes without saying! well done! bravo! Āp ke kyā kahne haiṅ! kyā jāne, kyā jāniye ; Illit kyā jane ; Rus. kenne ; Wom. ke jāne , Who knows; God knows; perhaps. Kyā jane, āve yā nahīṅ? kyā chīz hai, kyā khāk hai! intj. It's a mere nothing, a feather, a flea bite. Tumhāre āge voh kyā ckīz hai? kyā khūb , intj. 1. How pleasant, nice, beautiful! 2. What next! what a joke! Dont you wish you may get it? kyā gam hai! kyā muzāeqa! intj. What matter? What's the odds? It doesn't matter; never mind! kyā kyā , What not…
pron. What? how? why? whether, or. ¶ kīyā, part. past, or s. m. (from کرنا) Done, made, deed, act, doings.
subs: How fine! well done! bravo! &c. (see kyā-ḵẖūb): kyā bāt hai, It is a matter of no significance or importance; — it goes without saying, what need to mention it, not a doubt of it; — well done! bravo!
or kyā jāniye, What does one know? who knows? perhaps
or kyā ḵẖāk hai, What great thing is it? it's a mere nothing, or a mere trifle
How excellent! how fine! bravo! well done! what a joke! what next!

kyā — ki? utrum — an? is this the case or that?
(e.g. kyā īśvar patthar hai ki ātmā, 'is God a stone or (is He not rather) a spirit?)
What things? what things severally or respectively; what not; all sorts of

kyā maqdūr, kyā imkān, kyā tāb, or kyā iḵẖtiyār hai, What power has he? what can he do, or what dare he do? he dare not

def: s.m. corr. of لچها laććhā, q.v. s.m. (f. & dim., -ī), A bundle (of thread, &c.), a skein; a necklace of silk or beads (of glass or gold, fitting close to the neck); a bracelet (or an anklet) of two or more rows of chain-work; a tassel; — a thin shred or slice (as of ginger, or onion, &c.)
more: n. m. luchchī , n. f. S. तुच्छ See بدمعاش 1. A libertine; a rouè (اوباش). 2. A scamp; vagabond; reprobate. 3. A low abandoned fellow who leads a dissolute life and subsists by gambling, or by intimidating respectable persons to give him money. 4. A hubble-bubble; hukkā .

adj. 1. Loose; lewd; licentious ( aubāsh ). 2. Demoralized; corrupt; depraved ( burā , 8). 3. Shameless; graceless. 4. Wicked; naughty. 5. Given up to gambling, brawls, riots, etc. luchchā gunḍā . Loose characters.
s. m. A vagabond, a libertine, a rake, a profligate. adj. Low, mean.
s. m. (dakh.) Bankrupt ( Harris ): see تچا tuchchā.
subs: adj. Shred-like; entangled, involved, confused, jumbled; vague, desultory, roundabout; forming a string or chain, serial, consecutive (as words or speech).

ety: prob. S. यष्टिकः; cf. larćhā, and laṛī
def: s.m. (f. & dim., -ī), A bundle (of thread, &c.), a skein; a necklace of silk or beads (of glass or gold, fitting close to the neck); a bracelet (or an anklet) of two or more rows of chain-work; a tassel; — a thin shred or slice (as of ginger, or onion, &c.)
more: n. m. Dim. lachchhī . 1. A skein of thread. 2. A necklace of silk or of gold or glass beads. 3. A tassel. 4. Any thing cut up into thin shreds. 1. Adrak yā piyāz kā lachchhā . 2. Peṭhe kā lachchhā. lachchhe-dār , adj. 1. Shred-like. 2. Jumbled; confused; involved. 3. Roundabout; vague; desultory. 4. Serial; consecutive. Lachchhe-dār bāteṅ. lachhiyā'nā , v. a. To reel (thread, etc.).
s. m. A bundle (of thread), a ball or skein of thread, &c. ¶ (In the Dakhan ) A necklace worn tight round the neck, formed of gold beads and glass beads. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: adj. Shred-like; entangled, involved, confused, jumbled; vague, desultory, roundabout; forming a string or chain, serial, consecutive (as words or speech).

def: s.m. Distinctive mark, mark, sign, token, symbol, indication, characteristic, feature; description, definition; beauty; auspiciousness; trait, habit, manner, fashion, custom; state, condition (for other meanings see lakshaṅ)
more: n. m. S. लक्षण 1. See chinh , (1, 2). 2. Manner ( chāl , 6). Hāthoṅ meṅhdī, pāoṅ meṅhdī, apne lachchhan auroṅ deṅdī . Prov. Her hands with henna dyed, her feet with henna dyed, 'do as I do' she says. (When women apply meṅhdī they do no work till it dries). 3. State; condition. Kaho, wahāṅ ke kaise lachchhan haiṅ? lachchhan jhaṛnā , v. n. 1. To be disfigured, to lose one's beauty. 2. To degenerate; deteriorate; grow worse. 3. To be depraved, demoralized, corrupted. lachchhan sīkhnā, yā pakaṛnā , v. n. To learn manners.
s. m. Sign, mark; symptom, feature, auspiciousness (?). لچهن ونتا lachchh̤an-wantā (dakh.) Possessing marks; beautiful, fortunate, auspicious.
subs: or laććhan sīkhnā, To learn mauners
(-kī), 'The beauty (of a person) to fade,' to lose (one's) beauty; to be or become disfigured; — to degenerate, deteriorate; to become depraved, or demoralized, or corrupted.

def: s.m. A kind of grain, Cynosurus coracanus (called, by Europeans, 'Natchenny').
more: n. m. A kind of grain; Cynosurus coramus .

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of laksh, q.v.; also = lakshaṅ, q.v.

s.f. (dialec.) = lakshmī, q.v. s.f. Good fortune, good-luck, fortune, prosperity; wealth, riches; success, happiness; beauty, grace, loveliness, charm, splendour; — royal power, dominion; superhuman power; — a pearl; — name of the goddess of fortune and beauty (she is regarded as the wife of Vishṇu or Nārāyaṇ, and is one of the principal female deities of the Hindūs; she is also called Padmā, Kamalā and Śrī, and is supposed to be the Ceres of the ancients); — an epithet of Sītā, the wife of Rāma
more: s. Blood and water mixing: see, also, لسي lassi.
subs: s.m. 'The husband or lord of Lakshmī or Fortune,' a name of Vishṇu; lord or possessor of fortune or wealth, a wealthy man

s.f. 'The worship of Lakshmī,' name of a festival on the fifteenth day in the dark half of the month Kārtik, Sept.-Oct. (said to be celebrated, in modern times, by bankers and traders, to propitiate Fortune); — worship of Lakshmī by the bridegroom and bride after the bride has been brought to her father-in-law's house
s.f. 'Shadow (or protection or favour) of Lakshmī'; good fortune, prosperity, success
(kisī-ke) ghar-meṅ ānā or utarnā, 'Fortune to come to the house' (of); to enjoy good fortune or prosperity, to prosper, thrive; to have a run of good luck
(-ko), To offer (a meal, &c.) to Lakshmī and Nārāyaṇ before commencing (to eat, &c.); to begin to eat (syn. bismillāh karnā)
adj. (f. -vatī), Fortunate, prosperous, thriving; wealthy; handsome, beautiful.

v. n. 1. To be clammy, or glutinous. 2. To be elastick.

(f. -ā), Possessed of lucky signs or marks, lucky, fortunate, prosperous.
more: s. m. Name of the son of Dasaratha by Sumitrā, and half-brother of Rāmachandra.

subs: lit. 'To distribute cardamoms'; to invite to a marriage ceremony and feast (this being done, among Moḥammadan women, by sending cardamoms, betel-leaf, and sugar in a covered dish by the hands of a woman-servant, attended with music, to the female relatives whose presence is desired); — to form an attachment or intimacy
s.m. Capsule or pod of cardamoms
s.m. Cardamoms coated with sugar, comfits

s.m. A jolt, shove, push; a strain, a sprain; — a liking or taste (for); — gram pottage, &c. (see lāsā); — a barge, a pleasure boat; — gold or silver lace.
more: n. m. 1. Jolt; shove; push. 2. A sprain. Kamar meṅ lachkā ā gayā . 3. A barge; a pleasure boat. 4. Gold or silver lace.
s. m. 1. Jolt. 2. A barge, a kind of boat. ¶ part. Bent.
